consolidated_stakeholders.Role: Provides analysis on elderly employment trends and policies

Source metadata.filename: 2019_Elderly employment- latest developments in Hong Kong and policies in international perspective.pdf
Source metadata.extraction_context: This article examines the latest employment situation of elders aged 65 and above in Hong Kong in comparison to overseas economies. It also briefly reviews recent policies adopted in selected economies to promote elderly employment.

consolidated_stakeholders.Role: Introduced measures to require companies to extend the retirement age limits to no lower than age 60

Source metadata.filename: 2019_Elderly employment- latest developments in Hong Kong and policies in international perspective.pdf
Source metadata.extraction_context: In Korea, where there are no statutory but only firm-specific retirement age limits which are normally low, the government has introduced measures to require companies to extend the retirement age limits to no lower than age 60. In 2005, the government pursued the “wage peak” system to tackle early retirement practices...

consolidated_stakeholders.Role: Enacted the Employment Measure Act to prohibit age discrimination in recruitment and hiring

Source metadata.filename: 2019_Elderly employment- latest developments in Hong Kong and policies in international perspective.pdf
Source metadata.extraction_context: Similarly in Japan, the government enacted the Employment Measure Act in 2007 to prohibit age discrimination by firms in recruitment and hiring. Besides, the Law Concerning Stabilisation of Employment of Older Persons was amended in 2004 to require employers to take one of the following three measures...

all_sources.filename: 2019_Elderly employment- latest developments in Hong Kong and policies in international perspective.pdf

consolidated_stakeholders.Role: Provides a Special Employment Credit to employers who re-employ older workers

Source metadata.filename: 2019_Elderly employment- latest developments in Hong Kong and policies in international perspective.pdf
Source metadata.extraction_context: In Singapore, the minimum retirement age is statutorily set at age 62 under the Retirement and Re-employment Act (RRA), and the RRA stipulates that employers must offer re-employment to eligible employees who turn 62, up to age 67. Legislation aside, the Singaporean government also provides a Special Employment Credit to employers who re-employ older workers...

Source metadata.extraction_context: The caregiving journey may last for decades. Carers need to mobilise their resources and strengths, which can be leveraged in turn to strengthen their caregiving abilities and well-being.
